title: Episodes and Films (Portal)
wikitext:
{{laptop-frame|width=85%|max-width=100em
|stickers=
|screen_content=
{{EpisodeListHead}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number           = #01
| japanese_title_k = 使徒、襲来
| japanese_title_r = Shito Shuurai
| japanese_title_e = An Angel's Attack
| english_title    = [[Episode 01|Angel Attack]]
| image            = [[Image:Episode-01-Info.jpg|150px|Episode 01]]
| written_by       = [[Hideaki Anno]]
| directed_by      = [[Kazuya Tsurumaki]]
| first_aired      = 10-4-1995
| video_release    = 2-3-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 5-16-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels           = [[Sachiel]]
| eva_sorties      = [[Eva-01]]
| prev             = 
| next             = [[Episode 02]]
|description       = Shinji Ikari is summoned to Tokyo-3 by his estranged father Gendo, commander of the special agency Nerv. He must defend the city from a giant Angel by piloting the only weapon capable of battling the monster: Evangelion Unit-01. 
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number           = #02
| japanese_title_k = 見知らぬ、天井 
| japanese_title_r = Mishiranu Tenjou 
| japanese_title_e = Unfamiliar Ceiling 
| english_title    = [[Episode 02|The Beast]]
| image            = [[Image:Episode_02_info.jpg|150px|Episode 02]]
| written_by       = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Yoji Enokido]]
| directed_by      = [[Kazuya Tsurumaki]]
| first_aired      = 10-11-1995
| video_release    = 2-3-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 5-16-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels           = [[Sachiel]]
| eva_sorties      = Eva-01
| prev             = [[Episode 01]]
| next             = [[Episode 03]]
|description       = Shinji wakes up in the hospital, with no memory of defeating the Angel the night before. Rejected by his father, Misato decides to take Shinji with her to live in her apartment. Later that night, memories of the battle against the Angel come flooding back.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number           = #03
| japanese_title_k = 鳴らない、電話
| japanese_title_r = Naranai Denwa 
| japanese_title_e = The Phone That Doesn't Ring 
| english_title    = [[Episode 03| A Transfer]]
| image            = [[Image:Episode-03-info.jpg|150px|Episode 03]]
| written_by       = [[Akio Satsukawa]], [[Hideaki Anno]]
| directed_by      = [[Hiroyuki Ishido]]
| first_aired      = 10-18-1995
| video_release    = 3-6-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 5-16-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels           = [[Shamshel]]
| eva_sorties      = Eva-01
| prev             = [[Episode 02]]
| next             = [[Episode 04]]
|description       = Shinji begins attending his new school in Tokyo-3, and has a difficult time dealing with the fame of being an Evangelion pilot. Toji Suzuhara, who's little sister was injured in Shinji's fight against the Angel, is angry at him. A new Angel appears, and Shinji must once again pilot Eva-01 to defeat it.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number           = #04
| japanese_title_k =  雨、逃げ出した後
| japanese_title_r = Ame, Nigedashita Go
| japanese_title_e = Rain, After the Escape 
| english_title    = [[Episode 04|Hedgehog's Dilemma]]
| image            = [[Image:Shinjitrain.jpg|150px|Episode 04]]
| written_by       = [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by      = [[Tsuyoshi Kaga]]
| first_aired      = 10-25-1995
| video_release    = 3-6-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 5-16-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels           = None
| eva_sorties      = None
| prev             = [[Episode 03]]
| next             = [[Episode 05]]
| description      = Misato is upset with Shinji for ignoring her orders in the last Angel battle, and he is so overcome by the stress of being an Eva pilot that he runs away. After wandering around Tokyo-3 for several days, Shinji is faced with the choice of quitting or staying in his new home.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number           = #05
| japanese_title_k = レイ、心のむこうに
| japanese_title_r = Rei, Kokoro no Mukou ni 
| japanese_title_e = Beyond Rei's Heart 
| english_title    = [[Episode 05|Rei I]]
| image            = [[Image:Shinji_Rei_Escalator.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by       = [[Akio Satsukawa]], [[Hideaki Anno]]
| directed_by      = [[Keiichi Sugiyama]]
| first_aired      = 11-01-1995
| video_release    = 4-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 11-21-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels           = [[Ramiel]]
| eva_sorties      = Eva-01
| prev             = [[Episode 04]]
| next             = [[Episode 06]]
| description      = Eva-00 is finally repaired, and Shinji tries to get to know its enigmatic pilot, Rei Ayanami, better. However, he is confounded because she is extremely insular, has no friends at all, and the entire record of her past has been erased. In the midst of this, a very powerful Angel, the floating monolith Ramiel, attacks Tokyo-3.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number           = #06
| japanese_title_k =  決戦、第三新東京市
| japanese_title_r = Kessen, Daisan Shin Tokyo-shi
| japanese_title_e = Showdown in Tokyo-3
| english_title    = [[Episode 06|Rei II]]
| image            = [[Image:Japan_Blackout.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by       = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by      = [[Hiroyuki Ishido]]
| first_aired      = 11-08-1995
| video_release    = 4-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 11-21-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels           = [[Ramiel]]
| eva_sorties      = [[Eva-01]] and [[Eva-00]]
| prev             = [[Episode 05]]
| next             = [[Episode 07]]
| description      = Continuing from the previous episode, the Angel Ramiel is drilling down into the GeoFront to attack Nerv HQ directly. After Shinji barely survived a direct confrontation with it, Misato devises a plan to have Eva-00 and Eva-01 defeat the Angel by sniping it from a distance using a positron rifle which requires the total electric output of Japan to power up.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #07
| japanese_title_k = 人の造りしもの 
| japanese_title_r = Hito no Tsukurishimono 
| japanese_title_e = The Human Creation
| english_title    = [[Episode 07|A Human Work]]
| image = [[Image:07 C178 ohshi.jpg|150px|Episode 07]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Yoji Enokido]]
| directed_by = [[Keiichi Sugiyama]]
| first_aired = 11-15-1995
| video_release    = 5-2-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 11-21-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels    = 
| eva_sorties = Eva-01
| prev = [[Episode 06]]
| next = [[Episode 08]]
| description      =  Ritsuko briefs Shinji on the real nature of Second Impact, the Angels, and the Evangelions' mission to defeat them. Misato and Ritsuko attend the public demonstration of a new Angel-fighting robot, Jet Alone, built by a rival defense contractor to Nerv. However, the nuclear-powered robot quickly runs out of control, and Shinji must use Eva Unit 01 to load Misato onto Jet Alone so she can she can enter in its automatic shutdown code, before it melts down in a nuclear explosion.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #08
| japanese_title_k= アスカ、来日
| japanese_title_r = Asuka, Rainichi 
| japanese_title_e= Asuka Arrives in Japan
| english_title   = [[Episode 08|Asuka Strikes!]]
| image = [[Image:Asuka_Ships_Bridge.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Yoji Enokido]]
| directed_by = [[Kazuya Tsurumaki]]
| first_aired = 11-22-1995
| video_release    = 5-2-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 11-21-2000 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Gaghiel]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 07]]
| next = [[Episode 09]]
| description      =  Misato brings Shinji, along with Toji and Kensuke, to a massive UN naval convoy transporting Evangelion Unit-02 and its fiery German pilot, Asuka Langley Soryu, to Japan. Escorting Asuka is Misato's old boyfriend, Ryoji Kaji. After some awkward introductions and reunions, the fleet is attacked by a massive aquatic Angel. Asuka powers up Eva-02, and decides that she will defeat the Angel by playing "hopscotch"...
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #09
| japanese_title_k  = 瞬間、心、重ねて 
| japanese_title_r = Shunkan, Kokoro, Kasanete 
| japanese_title_e  = Movement, Heart, Together
| english_title   = [[Episode 09|Both of You, Dance Like You Want to Win! ]]
| image = [[Image: Dance_Vs_Israfel.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by = [[Seiji Mizushima]]
| first_aired = 11-29-1995
| video_release    = 6-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 1-23-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Israfel]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 08]]
| next = [[Episode 10]]
| description      =  Asuka joins Shinji’s class at school, and moves in with Misato and him in their apartment. As Asuka adjusts to Japan (or rather, everyone else adapts to her...), a new Angel attacks which is capable of splitting itself into two identical copies. Eva-01 and Eva-02 are defeated, and Nerv is publicly humiliated. With the Angel in a week-long regeneration period after an N² Mine attack by the JSSDF, Asuka and Shinji must learn to "synchronize" with each other's movements in combat to defeat it once and for all.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #10
| japanese_title_k  =  マグマダイバ
| japanese_title_r = MAGUMADAIBA
| japanese_title_e  = Magmadiver
| english_title   = [[Episode 10|Magma Diver]]
| image = [[Image: D-Type-Submerge.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by = [[Tsuyoshi Kaga]], [[Hiroyuki Ishido]]
| first_aired = 12-06-1995
| video_release    = 6-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 1-23-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Sandalphon]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 09]]
| next = [[Episode 11]]
| description      = The pilots are disappointed to find out that they are not allowed to go on their class trip to Okinawa, because they have to stay at Tokyo-3 to stay on-call in case of an Angel attack. Meanwhile, a new Angel is discovered in a chrysalis-like developmental stage deep within the magma of the volcano Mount Asama. Nerv seizes the opportunity to capture a live Angel for research; it is decided to send Eva-02 into the magma while wearing a super-cooled diving suit along with capture equipment. However, the Angel quickly matures and breaks containment, and Asuka is forced to battle it deep within the magma of the volcano.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #11
| japanese_title_k  = 静止した闇の中で
| japanese_title_r = Seishishita Yame no Naka de 
| japanese_title_e  = In the Still Darkness 
| english_title   = [[Episode 11|The Day Tokyo-3 Stood Still]]
| image = [[Image:Fuyutsuki_Candle.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Yoji Enokido]]
| directed_by = [[Tetsuya Watanabe]]
| first_aired = 12-13-1995
| video_release    = 7-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 1-23-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Matarael]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 10]]
| next = [[Episode 12]]
| description  = Tokyo-3 comes to a standstill when sabotage knocks out all power, and the personnel must scramble to launch the Evas manually when an Angel appears in the middle of the blackout.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #12
| japanese_title_k  =  奇跡の価値は
|japanese_title_r = Kiseki no Kachi wa
| japanese_title_e  = The Value of a Miracle is... 
| english_title   = [[Episode 12|She Said, "Don't Make Others Suffer for Your Personal Hatred."]]
| image = [[Image:Misatos_Intuition.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by = [[Hiroyuki Ishido]]
| first_aired = 12-20-1995
| video_release    = 7-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 2-13-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Sahaquiel]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 11]]
| next = [[Episode 13]]
| description = Misato has been promoted to Major, but the celebration does not last long. A monstrous Angel appears in Earth orbit right when Gendo and Fuyutsuki are away, leaving her and the children to deal with a colossal kamikaze threatening to drop itself right on top of Tokyo-3.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #13
| japanese_title_k  = 使徒、侵入
| japanese_title_r = Shito Shinnyuu 
| japanese_title_e  = Angel Invasion 
| english_title   = [[Episode 13|Lilliputian Hitcher]]
| image = [[Image:Ireul_Hacking_Magi.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]],[[Akio Satsukawa]], and [[Mitsuo Iso]]
| directed_by = [[Tensai Okamura]](director), [[Masahiko Otsuka]](assistant director)
| first_aired = 12-27-1995
| video_release    = 8-7-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 2-13-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Ireul]]
| eva_sorties = none
| prev = [[Episode 12]]
| next = [[Episode 14]]
| description = A routine test involving the Eva pilots turns into a struggle for survival when a wholly new type of Angel appears and spreads inside Nerv HQ. This new, computer-like angel threatens to set off the self-destruct for the entire facility, leaving Dr.Ritsuko Akagi in a race against time to save the GeoFront and destroy the Angel.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #14
| japanese_title_k =  ゼーレ、魂の座
| japanese_title_r = ZÊRE, Tamashii no Za
| japanese_title_e = SEELE, Throne of Souls 
| english_title   = [[Episode 14|Weaving a Story]]
| image = [[Image:Gendo_With_Committee.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]]
| directed_by = [[Masahiko Otsuka]], [[Ken Ando]]
| first_aired = 1-03-1996
| video_release    = 8-7-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 2-13-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = None (recap of all previous Angels)
| eva_sorties = none
| prev = [[Episode 13]]
| next = [[Episode 15]]
| description = This episode is in two parts: The first part being a clip show presented to the Human Instrumentality Committee of Seele, reviewing the attacks of all Angels up to this point. The second part includes a poem in which Rei contemplates her own existence, and a cross-compatibility test between Rei's and Shinji's Evas.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #15
| japanese_title_k = 嘘と沈黙
| japanese_title_r= Uso to Chinmoku
| japanese_title_e = Lies and Silence 
| english_title   = [[Episode 15|Those Women Longed for the Touch of Other's Lips, and Thus Invited Their Kisses]]
| image = [[Image:Visiting_Yuis_Grave.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by = [[Naoyasu Habu]]
| first_aired = 1-10-1996
| video_release    = 9-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 3-27-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Lilith]] (Brief appearance)
| eva_sorties = None
| prev = [[Episode 14]]
| next = [[Episode 16]]
| description = It seems like it's an important day for nearly everyone at Nerv. Misato and Ritsuko are going to a wedding; Shinji and Gendo are visiting Yui's grave; Asuka has a date. However, greater things will spring from these seemingly mundane events.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #16
| japanese_title_k = 死に至る病、そして
| japanese_title_r= Shi ni Itaru Yamai, Soshite
| japanese_title_e = Sickness unto death, and...
| english_title   = [[Episode 16|Splitting of the Breast]]
| image = [[image:16_C221_hell-train.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Hiroshi Yamaguchi]]
| directed_by = [[Kazuya Tsurumaki]]
| first_aired = 1-17-1996
| video_release    = 9-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 3-27-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Leliel]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 15]]
| next = [[Episode 17]]
| description = Shinji beats Asuka in a synch test, much to the latter's annoyance. Not too long after, the Evas are dispatched to deal with a mysterious apparition, and Eva-01 is sucked into a shadowlike blackness that materializes out of nothingness from under it. While an emergency operation to rescue the captured Eva is concocted, Shinji goes on a trip into the depths of his own consciousness...
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #17
| japanese_title_k = 四人目の適格者
| japanese_title_r = Yonninme no Tekikakusha
| japanese_title_e = The Fourth Qualified Individual
| english_title   = [[Episode 17|FOURTH CHILDREN]]
| image = [[Image:17 C153 shinji-kaji.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Shinji Higuchi]]
| directed_by = [[Minoru Ohara]]
| first_aired = 1-24-1996
| video_release    = 10-2-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 3-27-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = None
| eva_sorties = None
| prev = [[Episode 16]]
| next = [[Episode 18]]
| description = A Nerv branch in Nevada is obliterated in a catastrophic accident, and Nerv headquarters prepares to station a new Evangelion in Japan. The Fourth Child is selected, unbeknownst to Shinji.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #18
| japanese_title_k = 命の選択を
| japanese_title_r = Inochi no sentaku o
| japanese_title_e = A Life Choice
| english_title   = [[Episode 18|Ambivalence]]
| image = [[Image:18_C167_eva03-silhouette.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Shinji Higuchi]]
| directed_by = [[Tensai Okamura]]
| first_aired = 1-31-1996
| video_release    = 10-2-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 4-17-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Bardiel]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 17]]
| next = [[Episode 19]]
| description = Evangelion Unit-03 arrives in Japan from the United States. However, its activation experiment only continues the chain of misfortune begun with Eva-04 when it turns out to have been infected by the Thirteenth Angel. The other Evas are dispatched to ascertain the situation, and end up needing to battle the out-of-control Evangelion, even at the risk of killing its helpless pilot.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #19
| japanese_title_k = 男の戰い
| japanese_title_r = Otoko no Tatakai
| japanese_title_e = A Man's Battle
| english_title   = [[Episode 19|Introjection]]
| image = [[Image:19_C067_shinji-stern.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by = [[Masayuki]]
| first_aired = 2-7-1996
| video_release    = 12-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 4-17-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Zeruel]]
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 18]]
| next = [[Episode 20]]
| description = Enraged over Toji's near-death at the hands of his own Evangelion, Shinji refuses to pilot an Evangelion again and prepares to leave Tokyo-3 for good. Just as he is about to leave, an immensely destructive new Angel attacks and he is forced to reconsider his decision.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #20
| japanese_title_k = 心のかたち,人のかたち
| japanese_title_r = Kokoro no katachi, hito no katachi
| japanese_title_e = Form of the Mind, Form of the Man
| english_title   = [[Episode 20|Weaving a story 2: Oral Stage]]
| image = [[Image:20 C023 being-watched.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], 
| directed_by = [[Masahiko Otsuka]]
| first_aired = 2-14-1996
| video_release    = 12-5-1996 (Japan-Video/LD), 4-17-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = None
| eva_sorties = None
| prev = [[Episode 19]]
| next = [[Episode 21]]
| description = Shinji achieved an unprecedented 400% synchronization ratio with Eva-01 during the battle with Zeruel, to horrible effect. With the Eva having ascended to the status of a living God, and Shinji absorbed inside it, Ritsuko unsuccessfully attempts to recover him. In the meantime, Shinji makes contact with the resident soul of the Eva- his mother, Yui Ikari.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #21
| japanese_title_k = ネルフ、誕生
| japanese_title_r = Nerufu, tanjo
| japanese_title_e = The Birth of Nerv
| english_title   = [[Episode 21|He was aware that he was still a child]]
| image = [[Image:21 C181 path-to-god.jpg|150px|"The Path to God"]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by = [[Hiroyuki Ishido]]
| first_aired = 2-21-1996
| video_release    = 2-5-1997 (Japan-Video/LD), 5-29-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Adam]] (Flashback)
| eva_sorties = None
| prev = [[Episode 20]]
| next = [[Episode 22]]
| description = Kozo Fuyutsuki is kidnapped and interrogated by Seele, who are horrified over the results of the last encounter with an Angel. He recounts his past, providing flashbacks into the creation of Nerv. The pasts of Misato Katsuragi, Ritsuko Akagi, Rei Ayanami and Yui Ikari are also revealed. Ryoji Kaji, meanwhile, decides to find out the truth, no matter the cost.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #22
| japanese_title_k = せめて、人間らしく
| japanese_title_r = Semete, ningen rashiku
| japanese_title_e = At least, be human
| english_title   = [[Episode 22|Don't be.]]
| image = [[Image:22 C400 asuka-lonely.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Hiroshi Yamaguchi]]
| directed_by = [[Akira Takamura]]
| first_aired = 2-28-1996
| video_release    = 2-5-1997 (Japan-Video/LD), 5-29-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Arael]], [[Lilith]](Crucified)
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 21]]
| next = [[Episode 23]]
| description = Asuka's synch rate is on a downward spiral after her defeat at the hands of the Fourteenth Angel, Zeruel, and her frustration with Rei, Shinji, Misato and (unknown to her, dead) Kaji is on the rise. Angrier and more disturbed than ever, she is forced to relive her dark, traumatic past during the encounter with the Fifteenth Angel, Arael.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #23
| japanese_title_k = 涙
| japanese_title_r = Namida
| japanese_title_e = Tears
| english_title   = [[Episode 23|Rei III]]
| image = [[Image:23 C141 rei-namida.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Hiroshi Yamaguchi]]
| directed_by = [[Masahiko Otsuka]], [[Ken Ando]]
| first_aired = 3-6-1996
| video_release    = 3-5-1997 (Japan-Video/LD), 5-29-2001 (US-DVD)
| angels    = [[Armisael]], 
| eva_sorties = [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]], [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]]
| prev = [[Episode 22]]
| next = [[Episode 24]]
| description = Asuka sinks into deep depression after Arael's mental probing, and is unable to pilot when the new Angel Armisael arrives. Armisael begins physically fusing with Eva-00 and threatens to do the same to Eva-01, prompting Rei to sacrifice herself to save Shinji, only to appear alive soon afterwards. Under threat by Misato, Ritsuko opens the door to a room in Terminal Dogma to reveal the dark truth about Rei Ayanami.
}}
{{EpisodeListRow
| number = #24
| japanese_title_k = 最後のシ者
| japanese_title_r = Saigo no Shisha
| japanese_title_e = The Final Messenger
| english_title   = [[Episode 24|The Beginning and the End, or "Knockin' on Heaven's Door"]]
| image = [[Image:24 C308 deliberation.jpg|150px|Episode XX]]
| written_by = [[Hideaki Anno]], [[Akio Satsukawa]]
| directed_by = [[Masayuki]]
| first_aired = 3-13-1996
| video_release = 3-5-1997 (Japan-Video/LD), 6-26-2001 (US-DVD)

==Notes==

In the Episodes template below, you will see episodes organized in the Intro Arc (Eps 1-6), Action Arc (7-13), Descent Arc (14-20), Bitter End (21-24), and End of TV (the original series' ending in Eps 25-26), in addition to End of Evangelion's replacement ending, in Episodes 25' and 26'. This is a [https://web.archive.org/web/20060131070554/http://www.evacommentary.org/ long running fanon tradition] of organizing NGE's episodes, but it is '''not fully''' official. There is however, some allusion to it in official material, as the [https://www.evamonkey.com/platinum-booklets/episode-commentaries-01-06.php Platinum Episode Commentaries] do categorize the episodes in these sections, even if those arcs have no official name.

<br/>

{{Episode List}}

[[Category: Portals]]
html:
Image No. Title First Aired Description
#01 Angel Attack

使徒、襲来

10-4-1995 Shinji Ikari is summoned to Tokyo-3 by his estranged father Gendo, commander of the special agency Nerv. He must defend the city from a giant Angel by piloting the only weapon capable of battling the monster: Evangelion Unit-01.
#02 The Beast

見知らぬ、天井

10-11-1995 Shinji wakes up in the hospital, with no memory of defeating the Angel the night before. Rejected by his father, Misato decides to take Shinji with her to live in her apartment. Later that night, memories of the battle against the Angel come flooding back.
#03 A Transfer

鳴らない、電話

10-18-1995 Shinji begins attending his new school in Tokyo-3, and has a difficult time dealing with the fame of being an Evangelion pilot. Toji Suzuhara, who's little sister was injured in Shinji's fight against the Angel, is angry at him. A new Angel appears, and Shinji must once again pilot Eva-01 to defeat it.
#04 Hedgehog's Dilemma

雨、逃げ出した後

10-25-1995 Misato is upset with Shinji for ignoring her orders in the last Angel battle, and he is so overcome by the stress of being an Eva pilot that he runs away. After wandering around Tokyo-3 for several days, Shinji is faced with the choice of quitting or staying in his new home.
#05 Rei I

レイ、心のむこうに

11-01-1995 Eva-00 is finally repaired, and Shinji tries to get to know its enigmatic pilot, Rei Ayanami, better. However, he is confounded because she is extremely insular, has no friends at all, and the entire record of her past has been erased. In the midst of this, a very powerful Angel, the floating monolith Ramiel, attacks Tokyo-3.
#06 Rei II

決戦、第三新東京市

11-08-1995 Continuing from the previous episode, the Angel Ramiel is drilling down into the GeoFront to attack Nerv HQ directly. After Shinji barely survived a direct confrontation with it, Misato devises a plan to have Eva-00 and Eva-01 defeat the Angel by sniping it from a distance using a positron rifle which requires the total electric output of Japan to power up.
#07 A Human Work

人の造りしもの

11-15-1995 Ritsuko briefs Shinji on the real nature of Second Impact, the Angels, and the Evangelions' mission to defeat them. Misato and Ritsuko attend the public demonstration of a new Angel-fighting robot, Jet Alone, built by a rival defense contractor to Nerv. However, the nuclear-powered robot quickly runs out of control, and Shinji must use Eva Unit 01 to load Misato onto Jet Alone so she can she can enter in its automatic shutdown code, before it melts down in a nuclear explosion.
#08 Asuka Strikes!

アスカ、来日

11-22-1995 Misato brings Shinji, along with Toji and Kensuke, to a massive UN naval convoy transporting Evangelion Unit-02 and its fiery German pilot, Asuka Langley Soryu, to Japan. Escorting Asuka is Misato's old boyfriend, Ryoji Kaji. After some awkward introductions and reunions, the fleet is attacked by a massive aquatic Angel. Asuka powers up Eva-02, and decides that she will defeat the Angel by playing "hopscotch"...
#09 Both of You, Dance Like You Want to Win!

瞬間、心、重ねて

11-29-1995 Asuka joins Shinji’s class at school, and moves in with Misato and him in their apartment. As Asuka adjusts to Japan (or rather, everyone else adapts to her...), a new Angel attacks which is capable of splitting itself into two identical copies. Eva-01 and Eva-02 are defeated, and Nerv is publicly humiliated. With the Angel in a week-long regeneration period after an N² Mine attack by the JSSDF, Asuka and Shinji must learn to "synchronize" with each other's movements in combat to defeat it once and for all.
#10 Magma Diver

マグマダイバ

12-06-1995 The pilots are disappointed to find out that they are not allowed to go on their class trip to Okinawa, because they have to stay at Tokyo-3 to stay on-call in case of an Angel attack. Meanwhile, a new Angel is discovered in a chrysalis-like developmental stage deep within the magma of the volcano Mount Asama. Nerv seizes the opportunity to capture a live Angel for research; it is decided to send Eva-02 into the magma while wearing a super-cooled diving suit along with capture equipment. However, the Angel quickly matures and breaks containment, and Asuka is forced to battle it deep within the magma of the volcano.
#11 The Day Tokyo-3 Stood Still

静止した闇の中で

12-13-1995 Tokyo-3 comes to a standstill when sabotage knocks out all power, and the personnel must scramble to launch the Evas manually when an Angel appears in the middle of the blackout.
#12 She Said, "Don't Make Others Suffer for Your Personal Hatred."

奇跡の価値は

12-20-1995 Misato has been promoted to Major, but the celebration does not last long. A monstrous Angel appears in Earth orbit right when Gendo and Fuyutsuki are away, leaving her and the children to deal with a colossal kamikaze threatening to drop itself right on top of Tokyo-3.
#13 Lilliputian Hitcher

使徒、侵入

12-27-1995 A routine test involving the Eva pilots turns into a struggle for survival when a wholly new type of Angel appears and spreads inside Nerv HQ. This new, computer-like angel threatens to set off the self-destruct for the entire facility, leaving Dr.Ritsuko Akagi in a race against time to save the GeoFront and destroy the Angel.
#14 Weaving a Story

ゼーレ、魂の座

1-03-1996 This episode is in two parts: The first part being a clip show presented to the Human Instrumentality Committee of Seele, reviewing the attacks of all Angels up to this point. The second part includes a poem in which Rei contemplates her own existence, and a cross-compatibility test between Rei's and Shinji's Evas.
#15 Those Women Longed for the Touch of Other's Lips, and Thus Invited Their Kisses

嘘と沈黙

1-10-1996 It seems like it's an important day for nearly everyone at Nerv. Misato and Ritsuko are going to a wedding; Shinji and Gendo are visiting Yui's grave; Asuka has a date. However, greater things will spring from these seemingly mundane events.
#16 Splitting of the Breast

死に至る病、そして

1-17-1996 Shinji beats Asuka in a synch test, much to the latter's annoyance. Not too long after, the Evas are dispatched to deal with a mysterious apparition, and Eva-01 is sucked into a shadowlike blackness that materializes out of nothingness from under it. While an emergency operation to rescue the captured Eva is concocted, Shinji goes on a trip into the depths of his own consciousness...
#17 FOURTH CHILDREN

四人目の適格者

1-24-1996 A Nerv branch in Nevada is obliterated in a catastrophic accident, and Nerv headquarters prepares to station a new Evangelion in Japan. The Fourth Child is selected, unbeknownst to Shinji.
#18 Ambivalence

命の選択を

1-31-1996 Evangelion Unit-03 arrives in Japan from the United States. However, its activation experiment only continues the chain of misfortune begun with Eva-04 when it turns out to have been infected by the Thirteenth Angel. The other Evas are dispatched to ascertain the situation, and end up needing to battle the out-of-control Evangelion, even at the risk of killing its helpless pilot.
#19 Introjection

男の戰い

2-7-1996 Enraged over Toji's near-death at the hands of his own Evangelion, Shinji refuses to pilot an Evangelion again and prepares to leave Tokyo-3 for good. Just as he is about to leave, an immensely destructive new Angel attacks and he is forced to reconsider his decision.
#20 Weaving a story 2: Oral Stage

心のかたち,人のかたち

2-14-1996 Shinji achieved an unprecedented 400% synchronization ratio with Eva-01 during the battle with Zeruel, to horrible effect. With the Eva having ascended to the status of a living God, and Shinji absorbed inside it, Ritsuko unsuccessfully attempts to recover him. In the meantime, Shinji makes contact with the resident soul of the Eva- his mother, Yui Ikari.
#21 He was aware that he was still a child

ネルフ、誕生

2-21-1996 Kozo Fuyutsuki is kidnapped and interrogated by Seele, who are horrified over the results of the last encounter with an Angel. He recounts his past, providing flashbacks into the creation of Nerv. The pasts of Misato Katsuragi, Ritsuko Akagi, Rei Ayanami and Yui Ikari are also revealed. Ryoji Kaji, meanwhile, decides to find out the truth, no matter the cost.
#22 Don't be.

せめて、人間らしく

2-28-1996 Asuka's synch rate is on a downward spiral after her defeat at the hands of the Fourteenth Angel, Zeruel, and her frustration with Rei, Shinji, Misato and (unknown to her, dead) Kaji is on the rise. Angrier and more disturbed than ever, she is forced to relive her dark, traumatic past during the encounter with the Fifteenth Angel, Arael.
#23 Rei III

涙

3-6-1996 Asuka sinks into deep depression after Arael's mental probing, and is unable to pilot when the new Angel Armisael arrives. Armisael begins physically fusing with Eva-00 and threatens to do the same to Eva-01, prompting Rei to sacrifice herself to save Shinji, only to appear alive soon afterwards. Under threat by Misato, Ritsuko opens the door to a room in Terminal Dogma to reveal the dark truth about Rei Ayanami.
#24 The Beginning and the End, or "Knockin' on Heaven's Door"

最後のシ者

3-13-1996 Unable to find Asuka and too frightened to face Misato or Rei, Shinji runs away yet again, only to meet and strike a friendship with Kaworu Nagisa, the replacement pilot for Eva-02. Kaworu's openness and friendliness provide much-needed comfort for the disturbed Shinji. However, suspicions abound over Kaworu's incredible synchronization abilities, and when he reveals that he is the Seventeenth Angel, Shinji must battle his newfound friend or face the threat of world annihilation.
#25 Do you love me?

終わる世界

3-20-1996 Instrumentality has begun, and now its inner process, the joining of all souls into one, will be depicted. The episode is primarily driven by dialogue, the background usually complete darkness. White text often appears on a black screen, an omniscient voice questioning the characters.
#26 Take Care of Yourself

世界の中心でアイを叫んだけもの

3-27-1996 Instrumentality continues. The focus is primarily on Shinji now, as he learns to accept the individual’s ability to shape their world, and how the self cannot exist without others to define it.
#25' Love is destructive.

Air

7-19-1997 Depressed by his killing of Kaworu, Shinji slips into greater despair as Seele sends the MP Evas to destroy Nerv and take the Evangelion units by force. Meanwhile, Gendo attempts to use Rei to put his own plans into effect.
#26' I Need You.

まごころを、君に

7-19-1997 After being given the fate of humanity by Rei, Shinji decides that he is alone and unloved, choosing to begin Third Impact. As his internal contemplation continues, however, he begins to question whether or not Instrumentality is truly for the best.

Notes[edit]

In the Episodes template below, you will see episodes organized in the Intro Arc (Eps 1-6), Action Arc (7-13), Descent Arc (14-20), Bitter End (21-24), and End of TV (the original series' ending in Eps 25-26), in addition to End of Evangelion's replacement ending, in Episodes 25' and 26'. This is a long running fanon tradition of organizing NGE's episodes, but it is not fully official. There is however, some allusion to it in official material, as the Platinum Episode Commentaries do categorize the episodes in these sections, even if those arcs have no official name.


Episodes & Films
Television Series Intro Arc: 01 Angel Attack | 02 The Beast | 03 A Transfer | 04 Hedgehog's Dilemma | 05 Rei I | 06 Rei II

Action Arc: 07 A Human Work | 08 Asuka Strikes! | 09 Both of You, Dance Like You Want to Win! | 10 Magma Diver | 11 The Day Tokyo-3 Stood Still | 12 She said, 'Don’t make others suffer for your personal hatred.' | 13 Lilliputian Hitcher
Descent Arc: 14 Weaving a Story | 15 Those Women Longed for the Touch of Other's Lips, and Thus Invited Their Kisses | 16 Splitting of the Breast | 17 Fourth Children | 18 Ambivalence | 19 Introjection | 20 Weaving a story 2: Oral Stage
Bitter End Arc: 21 He was aware that he was still a child | 22 Don't be. | 23 Rei III | 24 The Beginning and the End, or "Knockin' on Heaven's Door"
End of TV: 25 Do You Love Me? | 26 Take Care of Yourself

Movies Death | Rebirth

The End of Evangelion: 25' Love is destructive. | 26' I Need You.

Rebuild of Evangelion Evangelion 1.0 | Evangelion 2.0 | Evangelion 3.0 | (-46h) | (-120min) | Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0
